# HubEx UI — AI reference

## Purpose

HubEx UI is the design system for HubEx plugins: a React component library
(`@cvetkov_es/react`), its stylesheet (`@cvetkov_es/css`), and the design tokens that
back both (`@cvetkov_es/tokens`). If you are an AI building UI for a HubEx plugin,
this file is the single reference for what exists and how to use it correctly
— read it before hand-rolling any button, input, table, modal, badge, etc.

**Derived from `@hubex/design-system@0.4.0` (themes.HubEx).** As of this
release, the source of truth for tokens and component APIs is the official
`@hubex/design-system` npm package (`themes.HubEx` branch), not Figma. Every
component listed under "Official-DS-backed components" below realigns its
props to that package's real TypeScript types (read from the vendored
reference at `tools/.official-ds-ref/package/dist/esm/src/components/`), with
documented, deliberate deviations only where this package's own constraints
require them (no styled-components runtime, no `@floating-ui/react`
dependency, Material-Icons-font glyphs instead of inline SVG — each is called
out on the component that has it). `docs/design-source/figma-variables.json`
and `docs/design-source/figma-variables.txt` are kept only as a **historical
record** of the earlier, hand-decoded Figma-variable source this system used
before the rebase — they are **not** the current source of truth and should
not be read for current token values or component behavior.

Every control below reproduces an existing pattern from the HubEx product; a
hand-rolled equivalent will visually and behaviorally diverge from the rest
of the system.

## Install

```bash
pnpm add @cvetkov_es/react @cvetkov_es/css
```

Both packages are required: `@cvetkov_es/react` ships components with **no**
CSS of their own (by design — this preserves tree-shaking, see Rules), and
`@cvetkov_es/css` ships the stylesheet those components' class names point at.

**Critical setup — do this once, at your app's entry point:**

```ts
// main.tsx / index.tsx — top level, once
import "@cvetkov_es/css";
```

This single import loads every `.hx-*` class and every `--hx-*` CSS custom
property for the whole app. Do **not** import `@cvetkov_es/css` more than once,
and do not import it from inside a component file — it belongs at the entry
point only.

**Icons:** the `Icon` component renders glyphs from the Material Icons
webfont by name (e.g. `<Icon name="home" />`) — it ships no font files and no
SVGs. Your app must load the Material Icons font itself and alias it to the
font-family `"material"` (e.g. Google's hosted webfont via a `<link>` tag, or
a self-hosted `@font-face` under that family name). If the font isn't loaded,
`Icon` renders the glyph name as plain text instead of the icon. Every other
component that takes an icon (`Button`'s `icon`/`endIcon`, `SegmentedControl`
option icons, `InputBase`'s `leftIcon`, etc.) is a Material glyph *name*
string too — this package never bundles or imports inline SVG (see the ban
documented on `Icon.tsx`); the official `@hubex/design-system` renders these
as SVG render-prop functions instead, which is the one deviation that recurs
across almost every component below.

## Design tokens

Every token below is a real CSS custom property, built by `@cvetkov_es/tokens` into
`packages/tokens/dist/variables.css` and loaded globally once you `import "@cvetkov_es/css"`.
**For any custom UI, use these variables (or the `.hx-*` classes below) — never
hardcode a hex value, a font stack, a radius, or a spacing number.** See
"Anti-invention rules" right after this section for the specific mistakes
this file's tables must never regress into.

There are **195 tokens total**, transcribed verbatim (same name, same value)
from `packages/tokens/dist/variables.css`, which is itself generated from
`@hubex/design-system`'s own `tokens.HubEx` object (see
`packages/tokens/src/tokens.json`, `packages/tokens/style-dictionary.config.mjs`).
Grouped below in the order the CSS file defines them. A handful of groups are
explicitly-declared **back-compat aliases** of a newer, larger scale — each is
labeled "(legacy alias)" and cross-references the scale it aliases; these
exist so pre-0.4.0 call sites referencing the old name keep working, not
because the value differs.

#### `color/text` (12)

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-color-text-primary` | `#1F1F1F` |
| `--hx-color-text-secondary` | `#777777` |
| `--hx-color-text-tertiary` | `#999999` |
| `--hx-color-text-neutral` | `#CCCCCC` |
| `--hx-color-text-subtle` | `#EEEEEE` |
| `--hx-color-text-inverse` | `#FFFFFF` |
| `--hx-color-text-weak` | `#FEFEFE` |
| `--hx-color-text-accent` | `#32B4EB` |
| `--hx-color-text-accent-hover` | `#70CBF1` |
| `--hx-color-text-success` | `#8DC26F` |
| `--hx-color-text-warning` | `#FF991F` |
| `--hx-color-text-error` | `#ED1940` |

#### `color/icon` (13)

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-color-icon-primary` | `#1F1F1F` |
| `--hx-color-icon-secondary` | `#777777` |
| `--hx-color-icon-tertiary` | `#999999` |
| `--hx-color-icon-neutral` | `#CCCCCC` |
| `--hx-color-icon-subtle` | `#EEEEEE` |
| `--hx-color-icon-inverse` | `#FFFFFF` |
| `--hx-color-icon-weak` | `#FEFEFE` |
| `--hx-color-icon-accent` | `#32B4EB` |
| `--hx-color-icon-accent-hover` | `#70CBF1` |
| `--hx-color-icon-success` | `#8DC26F` |
| `--hx-color-icon-warning` | `#FF991F` |
| `--hx-color-icon-error` | `#ED1940` |
| `--hx-color-icon-rating` | `#F9C901` |

#### `color/border` (11)

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-color-border-primary` | `#EEEEEE` |
| `--hx-color-border-secondary` | `#999999` |
| `--hx-color-border-tertiary` | `#777777` |
| `--hx-color-border-neutral` | `#CCCCCC` |
| `--hx-color-border-inverse` | `#FFFFFF` |
| `--hx-color-border-weak` | `#FEFEFE` |
| `--hx-color-border-accent` | `#32B4EB` |
| `--hx-color-border-accent-hover` | `#70CBF1` |
| `--hx-color-border-success` | `#8DC26F` |
| `--hx-color-border-warning` | `#FF991F` |
| `--hx-color-border-error` | `#ED1940` |

#### `color/background` (16)

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-color-background-primary` | `#353535` |
| `--hx-color-background-secondary` | `#777777` |
| `--hx-color-background-tertiary` | `#999999` |
| `--hx-color-background-strong` | `#484856` |
| `--hx-color-background-neutral` | `#CCCCCC` |
| `--hx-color-background-subtle` | `#EEEEEE` |
| `--hx-color-background-weak` | `#F8F8F8` |
| `--hx-color-background-page` | `#F8F8F8` |
| `--hx-color-background-hoverlist` | `#F8F8FA` |
| `--hx-color-background-inverse` | `#FFFFFF` |
| `--hx-color-background-accent` | `#32B4EB` |
| `--hx-color-background-accent-hover` | `#70CBF1` |
| `--hx-color-background-highlight` | `#E8F7FD` |
| `--hx-color-background-success` | `#8DC26F` |
| `--hx-color-background-warning` | `#FF991F` |
| `--hx-color-background-error` | `#ED1940` |

*Note: `--hx-color-background-error` and `--hx-color-background-warning` are
misspelled in the official `@hubex/design-system` source as
`color-backgroundg-error`/`-warning` (an extra "g") — confirmed present in
the vendored package itself, not just the old Figma export. That typo is
corrected here so the tokens are reachable by their expected name. As with
every error hue, these are for error/warning surfaces (e.g. `BadgeCount`,
`BadgeDot`), **never** a button fill — there is no red button.*

#### `color/menu` (4)

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-color-menu-background-default` | `#353535` |
| `--hx-color-menu-background-hover` | `#39393E` |
| `--hx-color-menu-background-active` | `#242324` |
| `--hx-color-menu-background-accent` | `#ED1940` |

#### `color/overlay` (1)

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-color-overlay` | `rgba(0,0,0,0.5)` |

#### `color/avatar` (27)

Backs the `Avatar` component's 9 auto-selected colour sets (see the `Avatar`
entry below) — not meant to be reached for directly in custom UI, but listed
here because they're real tokens like any other.

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-color-avatar-one-text` | `#ED1940` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-one-border` | `#ED1940` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-one-background` | `#FDE8EC` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-two-text` | `#FF991F` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-two-border` | `#FF991F` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-two-background` | `#FFF5E8` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-three-text` | `#F9C901` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-three-border` | `#F9C901` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-three-background` | `#FEF9E5` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-four-text` | `#00A861` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-four-border` | `#00A861` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-four-background` | `#E5F6EF` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-five-text` | `#3FCE1B` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-five-border` | `#3FCE1B` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-five-background` | `#EBFAE8` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-six-text` | `#1243BF` |
| `--hx-color-avatar-six-border` | `#1243BF` |

#### `font/family` (1)

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-font-family` | `Roboto, Helvetica, sans-serif` |

#### `font/heading` — H0–H3 (16)

Each heading step defines its own size/weight/line-height/family sub-tokens
(`Text` component's `font-H0`…`font-H3` variants read these).

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-font-h0-size` | `24px` |
| `--hx-font-h0-weight` | `500` |
| `--hx-font-h0-line-height` | `28px` |
| `--hx-font-h0-family` | `Roboto, Helvetica, sans-serif` |
| `--hx-font-h1-size` | `20px` |
| `--hx-font-h1-weight` | `500` |
| `--hx-font-h1-line-height` | `26px` |
| `--hx-font-h1-family` | `Roboto, Helvetica, sans-serif` |
| `--hx-font-h2-size` | `18px` |
| `--hx-font-h2-weight` | `500` |
| `--hx-font-h2-line-height` | `24px` |
| `--hx-font-h2-family` | `Roboto, Helvetica, sans-serif` |
| `--hx-font-h3-size` | `16px` |
| `--hx-font-h3-weight` | `500` |
| `--hx-font-h3-line-height` | `22px` |
| `--hx-font-h3-family` | `Roboto, Helvetica, sans-serif` |

| `--hx-border-radius-medium` | `5px` |
| `--hx-border-radius-circle` | `50%` |
| `--hx-border-radius-pill` | `9999px` |

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-border-solid-style` | `solid` |
| `--hx-border-dashed-style` | `dashed` |

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|

| `--hx-spacing-x250percent` | `10px` |
| `--hx-spacing-x3` | `12px` |
| `--hx-spacing-x350percent` | `14px` |
| `--hx-spacing-x4` | `16px` |
| `--hx-spacing-x450percent` | `18px` |

| `--hx-spacing-x14` | `56px` |
| `--hx-spacing-x15` | `60px` |
| `--hx-spacing-x16` | `64px` |

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-size-x05` | `2px` |
| `--hx-size-x1` | `4px` |
| `--hx-size-x150percent` | `6px` |
| `--hx-size-x2` | `8px` |
| `--hx-size-x250percent` | `10px` |
| `--hx-size-x3` | `12px` |

#### `shadow` (3)

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-shadow-base` | `0px 0px 12px 0px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)` |
| `--hx-shadow-colored` | `0px 0px 6px 0px rgba(97,168,199,0.15)` |
| `--hx-shadow-actionpanel` | `0px 2px 6px 0px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)` |

#### `radius` (legacy alias of `border-radius-small`/`-medium`/`-pill`) (3)

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `--hx-radius-small` | `3px` |
| `--hx-radius-medium` | `5px` |
| `--hx-radius-pill` | `9999px` |

## Anti-invention rules (read before writing ANY UI)

This design system was originally re-based in 0.2.0 on hand-decoded Figma
variables (replacing an even earlier 0.1.0 set that had invented
plausible-looking tokens and values that did not exist in the actual
design), and is now re-based again on the official `@hubex/design-system`
package itself — a strictly higher-fidelity ground truth than a hand-decoded
`.fig` export. The rules below are the specific, recurring mistakes that
caused the *original* 0.1.0 defect class — they still apply, because the
underlying design facts they describe (no red button, pill radius, real
token values) haven't changed, only the source they're read from:

- **`#ED1940` (the `*-error` tokens: `--hx-color-text-error`,
  `--hx-color-icon-error`, `--hx-color-border-error`,
  `--hx-color-background-error`) is for the text, icons, and borders of an
  error state — NEVER a button fill.** There is no red/"danger" button
  anywhere in HubEx. `ButtonProps["variant"]` has no `"danger"` option (see
  `Button` below) — destructive actions (disable, revoke, delete, etc.) use a
  neutral **secondary** (frameless, grey-on-hover) button, exactly like any
  other secondary action. Do not reach for a red button "just in case".
- **Buttons are pill-shaped: the base `.hx-btn` uses `--hx-border-radius-pill`
  (`9999px`, also reachable via the legacy `--hx-radius-pill` alias) for
  `primary`/`secondary`/`ghost`.** Never invent a button radius. The one real
  exception: the `dashed` variant uses `--hx-border-radius-small` (`3px`).
- **Never hardcode a hex value or a raw px number that isn't one of the 195
  tokens above.** If a value you need doesn't map to an existing token,
  that's a signal to double check `packages/tokens/dist/variables.css` (or
  ask) before inventing a one-off number — do not guess a "close enough"
  value.
- **Never invent a component prop.** Every prop documented below was read
  directly from the component's own `.tsx` interface in
  `packages/react/src/<Name>/<Name>.tsx` — if a prop you want isn't listed
  here, it does not exist; check the source file yourself before assuming.

## Component index

All 40 components are named exports of `@cvetkov_es/react`. Every component
that renders a native HTML element also merges any `className` you pass with
its own `.hx-*` classes (either by extending the matching
`React.*HTMLAttributes` interface and spreading the rest, or — for the
handful of components with an explicit, non-spread API, called out
individually below — by declaring `className?: string` itself). Props shown
below are copied verbatim from each component's own TypeScript interface;
where a component deviates from the official `@hubex/design-system` API
(e.g. Material glyph *names* instead of SVG render props), that deviation is
called out inline exactly as commented in the source.

27 components below are **official-DS-backed**: their props are realigned to
`@hubex/design-system@0.4.0`'s own component types. The remaining 13 are
**unofficial extensions** — this package's own additions with no
`@hubex/design-system` counterpart — clearly marked in their own section
further down.

### Official-DS-backed components

### Button
`ButtonProps extends React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>`
- `variant?: "primary" | "secondary" | "ghost" | "dashed"` (default `"primary"`)
- `size?: "small" | "medium" | "sm" | "md"` (default `"medium"`) — the official
  API renamed `sm`/`md` to `small`/`medium`; both old and new names are
  accepted at runtime (`sm`→`small`, `md`→`medium`) so 0.1.x/0.2.x call sites
  keep working.
- `round?: boolean` — circular icon button (`--hx-size-button-circle-s/m`
  instead of the pill shape).
- `loading?: boolean` — replaces content with a spinner and disables the button.
- `fullWidth?: boolean` — stretches to 100% of the container (ignored when `round`).
- `error?: string` — recolors the `dashed` variant's border to the error
  token and renders a caption-sized message below the button.
- `icon?: string`, `endIcon?: string` — **DEVIATION**: the official `icon`/
  `endIcon` are SVG render props; here they are Material Icons glyph *names*
  rendered as `<span class="material">{name}</span>`.
- `iconColor?: "color-icon-accent" | "color-icon-secondary"` — recolors `icon`/`endIcon`; ignored when neither is set.
- `ariaLabel?: string` — accessible name override; also settable via native `aria-label`.

`secondary` and `ghost` render the SAME look: frameless, transparent at rest,
light-grey fill (`--hx-color-background-subtle`, `#EEEEEE`) on hover — no
border in any state. They are kept as aliases; pick whichever reads better.
`primary` is the filled accent button; `dashed` is the add/placeholder
outline. No `"danger"` variant exists — the type checker rejects it.
```tsx
<Button variant="secondary" size="small" onClick={handleDisable}>Disable</Button>
<Button variant="dashed" icon="add">Add</Button>
<Button loading>Saving…</Button>
```

### Input
`InputProps extends Omit<React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "type" | "onChange">`
- `type?: "text" | "password" | "email" | "tel" | "url" | "search" | "number"` (default `"text"`)
- `label?: string`
- `errorText?: string`, `errorId?: string`
- `allowClear?: boolean`
- `textLimit?: number` (maps to native `maxLength`, taking precedence over a raw `maxLength` prop)
- `fullWidth?: boolean`
- `ariaLabel?: string`, `ariaLabelledBy?: string`, `ariaDescribedBy?: string`, `ariaControls?: string`, `ariaExpanded?: boolean`
- `onChange?: (event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void`
- `invalid?: boolean` — legacy (0.1.x/0.2.x) back-compat alias; drives the same error styling as `errorText` presence.
```tsx
<Input label="Email" placeholder="you@hubex" allowClear errorText={emailError} />
```

### InputBase
`InputBaseProps extends Omit<React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "onChange" | "value" | "maxLength" | "role">`
- `name?: string`, `id?: string`
- `value: string` (required — always controlled)
- `onChange?: (event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void`
- `placeholder?: string`, `required?: boolean`, `disabled?: boolean`, `fullWidth?: boolean`
- `errorText?: string`, `hideErrorText?: boolean`, `errorId?: string`, `maxLength?: number`
- `leftIcon?: React.ReactNode` — **DEVIATION**: official `leftIcon` is an SVG render prop; here it's a plain `ReactNode` (e.g. `<Icon name="search" />`).
- `rightSlot?: React.ReactNode` — right-side affordance (chevron, clear button, lock, …); compose multiple yourself.
- `rightSlotWidthPx?: number` (default `36`) — right padding reserved for `rightSlot`.
- `type?: string` (default `"text"`)
- `onFocusChange?: (isFocused: boolean) => void`
- `role?: string`, `ariaLabel?: string`, `ariaLabelledBy?: string`, `ariaDescribedBy?: string`, `ariaControls?: string`, `ariaExpanded?: boolean`, `ariaHasPopup?: boolean | "dialog" | "menu" | "listbox" | "grid" | "false" | "true" | "tree"`, `ariaActivedescendant?: string`, `ariaAutocomplete?: "none" | "list" | "both"`

This is the low-level primitive `Search` and `Select`'s trigger are built on
— reach for it directly only when neither `Input` nor `Search` fits.
```tsx
<InputBase value={query} onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)} leftIcon={<Icon name="person" />} />
```

### Search
`SearchProps extends Omit<React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "onChange" | "value" | "maxLength" | "required" | "disabled">`
- `value: string` (required — always controlled)
- `onChange?: (event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void`
- `placeholder?: string`, `fullWidth?: boolean`, `errorText?: string`, `name?: string`
- `ariaLabel?: string` (defaults to `ariaLabel ?? placeholder ?? "Search"`)

Built on `InputBase`; always renders a leading search glyph and a clear
button (visibility-toggled, not conditionally rendered, so layout never shifts).
```tsx
<Search value={query} onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)} placeholder="Search plugins" />
```

### TextArea
`TextAreaProps extends React.TextareaHTMLAttributes<HTMLTextAreaElement>`
- `label?: string`
- `textLimit?: number` (maps to native `maxLength`)
- `errorText?: string`
- `allowClear?: boolean` — shows a clear (×) button whenever the field has a value.
- `autoSize?: boolean` — height grows/shrinks with content (`resize: none`).
- `maxRows?: number` — with `autoSize`, caps height (~rows); extra content scrolls.
```tsx
<TextArea label="Notes" autoSize maxRows={6} textLimit={500} allowClear />
```

### Checkbox
`CheckboxProps extends React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>`
- `label?: string`
- `indeterminate?: boolean` — no native HTML attribute/React prop equivalent; set imperatively on the DOM node via a merged ref.
- `ariaLabel?: string`
```tsx
<Checkbox label="I agree" checked={agreed} onChange={(e) => setAgreed(e.target.checked)} />
<Checkbox indeterminate label="Select all" />
```

### Radio
`RadioProps extends Omit<React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "type">`
- `label?: React.ReactNode` — a plain string is fine; `ReactNode` is supported for richer markup.
- `ariaLabel?: string`
```tsx
<Radio name="plan" label="Pro" value="pro" checked={plan === "pro"} onChange={() => setPlan("pro")} />
```

### RadioGroup
`RadioGroupProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "onChange">`
- `name: string` (required — shared across every `Radio` in the group)
- `value?: string`, `defaultValue?: string`
- `onChange?: (value: string, event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void`
- `options?: RadioGroupOption[]` where `RadioGroupOption = { value: string; label: React.ReactNode; disabled?: boolean; id?: string }`
- `direction?: "row" | "column"` (default `"column"`)
- `disabled?: boolean` — disables every option in the group.
- `ariaLabel?: string`
```tsx
<RadioGroup
  name="plan"
  value={plan}
  onChange={setPlan}
  direction="row"
  options={[{ value: "free", label: "Free" }, { value: "pro", label: "Pro" }]}
/>
```

### Toggle
`ToggleProps extends Omit<React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "onChange" | "checked">`
- `name: string` (required — Toggle is backed by a real `<input type="checkbox" role="switch">`)
- `label?: string`
- `checked?: boolean` (default `false`)
- `disabled?: boolean`
- `ariaLabel?: string`
- `onChange?: (checked: boolean, event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void` — **BREAKING (post-0.2.x)**: signature gained a second `event` parameter (matching the official `@hubex/design-system` Toggle API); the boolean is still first, so callers that only used the first argument keep working, but a bare `onChange={setState}` where `setState` requires a *single* argument (rare — most `useState` setters ignore extras) must add the second parameter or ignore it.
```tsx
<Toggle name="notifications" checked={enabled} onChange={(checked) => setEnabled(checked)} />
```

### Select
`SelectProps<TOption extends SelectOption = SelectOption> extends Omit<React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "value" | "defaultValue" | "onChange" | "onBlur" | "type" | "role" | "size">`

**BREAKING (0.3.x+): Select is now a self-contained combobox**, not a styled
native `<select>` — the old `<Select><option/></Select>` children-based API
is gone.
- `options: TOption[]` (required) where `SelectOption = { value: string; label: string; disabled?: boolean }`
- `value?: string`, `defaultValue?: string`
- `onChange?: (value: string, option: TOption | null) => void`
- `onQueryChange?: (query: string) => void` — fires on filter-typing, including when cleared.
- `renderOption?: (option: TOption, state: SelectOptionState) => React.ReactNode` where `SelectOptionState = { active: boolean; selected: boolean; disabled: boolean }`
- `filterBy?: (option: TOption, searchQuery: string) => boolean` — `searchQuery` is always `inputText.trim().toLowerCase()`; not called when empty. Default: substring match on `label`.
- `allowClear?: boolean`
- `actions?: SelectActionItem<TOption>[]` where `SelectActionItem = { icon: string; ariaLabel: string; onClick: (option, event) => void; disabled?: boolean }` — **DEVIATION**: official `icon` is an SVG render prop; here it's a Material glyph name. Rendered in a footer row of the open menu (the official API renders them inline in the trigger row — simplified here).
- `label?: string`, `required?: boolean`, `disabled?: boolean`
- `onBlur?: (event: React.FocusEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void`
- `invalid?: boolean` — legacy back-compat alias for error styling.

Self-contained (no `floating-ui`, no portal — CSS `position: absolute` menu).
```tsx
<Select
  label="Country"
  options={[{ value: "us", label: "US" }, { value: "de", label: "DE" }]}
  value={country}
  onChange={(value) => setCountry(value)}
  allowClear
/>
```

### Pagination
`PaginationProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>, "onChange">`

**BREAKING (0.3.x): rewritten to the official item-count-driven API** —
the old `{page, pageCount, onPageChange}` (prev/next arrows, no page-size
control) is gone; the official surface has no prev/next arrows at all.
- `totalItems: number` (required)
- `page?: number` (controlled, 1-indexed; out-of-range values are clamped for display, `onChange` reports the clamped page)
- `defaultPage?: number` (default `1`, uncontrolled)
- `pageSize?: number` (controlled)
- `defaultPageSize?: number` (default `25`, uncontrolled)
- `onChange?: (payload: PaginationChangePayload) => void` where `PaginationChangePayload = { page: number; pageSize: number; offset: number }` (`offset = (page - 1) * pageSize`)
- `pageSizeLabel?: string` (default `"Content"`)
- `pageSizeOptions?: number[]` (default `[25, 50, 100]`)
- `hidePageSizeSelector?: boolean`
- `buildItemsOptions?: BuildPaginationItemsOptions` where `BuildPaginationItemsOptions = { edgeCount?: number /* default 5 */; siblingCount?: number /* default 2 */; compactThreshold?: number /* default 7 */ }`
- `disabled?: boolean`
- `ariaLabel?: string` (default `"Pagination"`)
- `pageSizeAriaLabel?: string` (default `pageSizeLabel`)
- `getPageAriaLabel?: (page: number) => string` (default `` `Page ${page}` ``)
```tsx
<Pagination
  totalItems={162}
  page={page}
  pageSize={pageSize}
  onChange={({ page, pageSize }) => { setPage(page); setPageSize(pageSize); }}
/>
```

### Tooltip
`TooltipProps`: `{ content: string; children: React.ReactElement<Record<string, unknown>>; placement?: "top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right" /* default "bottom" */ }`
(`content` is text only, matching the official API — no rich content.)
```tsx
<Tooltip content="Delete this item">
  <Button onClick={handleDelete}>Delete</Button>
</Tooltip>
```

### Popover
`PopoverProps`:
- `content: React.ReactNode` (required)
- `children: React.ReactElement<Record<string, unknown>>` (required — single trigger element, cloned)
- `placement?: "top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right"` (default `"bottom"`) — **DEVIATION**: official uses `@floating-ui/react`'s 12-value `Placement` (incl. `-start`/`-end`); this CSS-positioned implementation narrows to the 4 cardinal directions.
- `trigger?: "click" | "hover" | "click-hover"` (default `"click"`)
- `hoverDelay?: number | { open?: number; close?: number }` — also used as the `closeOnPointerLeave` grace period; falls back to 150ms.
- `closeOnPointerLeave?: boolean` (default `false`) — click-mode only: also close when pointer leaves trigger+panel.
- `size?: "m" | "l"` (default `"m"`)
- `maxWidth?: number | string`, `maxHeight?: number | string`
- `open?: boolean`, `onOpenChange?: (open: boolean) => void`
- `disabled?: boolean`
- `ariaLabel?: string`, `ariaLabelledBy?: string`
- `role?: string | null` — INTERNAL EXTENSION (not in the official API), default `"dialog"`; `null` omits the role attribute. Lets `Dropdown`/`Info` reuse this same panel with their own role.
- `panelClassName?: string` — INTERNAL EXTENSION; extra class(es) merged onto the panel.

**OMITTED vs. the official floating-ui-based Popover** (documented on the
component itself): no auto-flip/`fallbackPlacements`, no portal
(`portalContainer` unsupported — the panel can be clipped by an
`overflow: hidden` ancestor), no arrow/caret.
```tsx
<Popover content="Extra detail" placement="right">
  <Button variant="secondary">Details</Button>
</Popover>
```

### Dropdown
`DropdownProps`:
- `children: React.ReactElement<Record<string, unknown>>` (required — trigger, cloned)
- `content: React.ReactNode` (required)
- `placement?: "top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right"` (default `"bottom"` — official default is `"bottom-start"`, an alignment variant this CSS-positioned build doesn't support)
- `trigger?: "click" | "hover" | "click-hover" | "manual"` (default `"click"`) — `"manual"` disables all built-in open/close handling; visibility is driven solely by `open`.
- `role?: "menu" | "listbox" | "dialog" | "none"` (default `"menu"`)
- `ariaHasPopup?: "menu" | "listbox" | "dialog" | "grid" | "tree" | "true"` (defaults to `role`, or `"dialog"` when `role="none"`)
- `applyTriggerAria?: boolean` (default `true`) — sets `aria-haspopup`/`aria-expanded` on the trigger.
- `open?: boolean`, `onOpenChange?: (open: boolean) => void`
- `disabled?: boolean`
- `ariaLabel?: string`, `ariaLabelledBy?: string`
- `minWidth?: number | string`, `maxHeight?: number | string`

Built entirely on `Popover` — inherits every omission documented there.
`matchTriggerWidth` and `portalContainer` are OMITTED.
```tsx
<Dropdown content={<MyMenuList />}>
  <Button variant="secondary">Actions</Button>
</Dropdown>
```

### Info
`InfoProps`:
- `content: React.ReactNode` (required)
- `placement?: "top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right"` (default `"bottom"`)
- `maxWidth?: number | string`, `maxHeight?: number | string`
- `open?: boolean`, `onOpenChange?: (open: boolean) => void`
- `trigger?: "click" | "hover" | "click-hover"` (default `"click"`)
- `hoverDelay?: number | { open?: number; close?: number }`
- `closeOnPointerLeave?: boolean` (default `false`)
- `ariaLabel?: string` (default `"Information"` — the official default is the Russian "Информация", translated to match this codebase's English-only-defaults convention)
- `popoverAriaLabel?: string`, `popoverAriaLabelledBy?: string`

Built on `Popover`. **DEVIATION**: the official trigger is a bespoke inline
SVG "i" glyph; here it's `<Icon name="info" />` (Material Icons).
```tsx
<Info content="This value updates automatically." />
```

### SegmentedControl
`SegmentedControlProps`:
- `options: SegmentedControlOption[]` (required) where `SegmentedControlOption = { value: string; label: React.ReactNode; icon?: string; disabled?: boolean; actionIcon?: SegmentedControlActionIcon }`
- `value?: string`, `defaultValue?: string` (falls back to the first non-disabled option)
- `onChange?: (value: string) => void`
- `compact?: boolean` (default `false`) — icon-only rendering; labels dropped from the DOM, exposed only via `aria-label`. **DEVIATION**: the official `compact` also auto-activates via `ResizeObserver` and wraps segments in a floating-ui `Tooltip`; both are OMITTED here (self-contained, no `ResizeObserver`, no runtime `Tooltip` dependency) — `compact` is purely an explicit caller-forced flag.
- `disabled?: boolean`
- `ariaLabel?: string`, `ariaLabelledby?: string`, `ariaDescribedby?: string`
- `className?: string`

`SegmentedControlActionIcon = { icon: string; iconColor?: IconColor; ariaLabel: string; onClick: (event) => void; disabled?: boolean }`
— **DEVIATION**: official `icon` is an SVG render prop; here it's a Material
glyph name. Shown only on the selected segment; does not change selection.
```tsx
<SegmentedControl
  value={range}
  onChange={setRange}
  options={[{ value: "day", label: "Day" }, { value: "week", label: "Week" }, { value: "month", label: "Month" }]}
/>
```

### Text
`TextProps`:
- `variant: "font-H0" | "font-H1" | "font-H2" | "font-H3" | "font-body-regular" | "font-body-medium" | "font-paragraph-regular" | "font-paragraph-medium" | "font-tooltip-regular" | "font-tooltip-medium" | "font-caption-regular"` (required)
- `children: React.ReactNode` (required)
- `color?: "color-text-inverse" | "color-text-subtle" | "color-text-error" | "color-text-secondary" | "color-text-primary" | "color-text-neutral" | "color-text-weak" | "color-text-accent"` (omitting falls back to `--hx-color-text-primary`)
- `ellipsis?: boolean`
- `className?: string`

Always renders a `<p>` (the vendored bundle's `TextRoot` is `styled.p` for
every variant, including the `paragraph-*` ones — no per-variant element switch).
```tsx
<Text variant="font-H2">Section title</Text>
<Text variant="font-body-regular" color="color-text-secondary">Helper copy</Text>
```

### Link
`LinkProps`:
- `variant?: TextVariant` (default `"font-body-regular"` — same union as `Text`'s `variant`)
- `color?: TextColor` (same union as `Text`'s `color`; default resolves to `"color-text-accent"`, forced to `"color-text-subtle"` when `disabled`, overriding any explicit `color`)
- `ellipsis?: boolean`
- `children: React.ReactNode` (required)
- `href?: string`
- `target?: "_blank" | "_self" | "_parent" | "_top"`
- `rel?: string`
- `external?: boolean` — forces `target="_blank"` even over an explicit `target`; an explicit `rel` always wins, otherwise defaults to `"noopener noreferrer"` when the resolved target is `"_blank"`.
- `onClick?: (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLAnchorElement>) => void`
- `disabled?: boolean` — swallows clicks (`preventDefault`+`stopPropagation`); forces `color-text-subtle`; a disabled link with no `href` gets `role="link"` restored (loses implicit link semantics otherwise).
- `ariaLabel?: string`
- `dataID?: string` (rendered as `data-id`)
- `className?: string` — house convention; not part of the official `LinkProps`.

Typography/color/truncation share the exact same `.hx-text--*` modifier
classes as `Text` (the official DS composes Link's styling directly off
`TextProps` too).
```tsx
<Link href="https://example.com" external>Open documentation</Link>
```

### Loader
`LoaderProps`:
- `size: "small" | "medium" | "large"` (required — no default in the official API)
- `color: "color-icon-primary" | "color-icon-secondary" | "color-icon-error"` (required — no default)
- `className?: string` — house convention; not part of the official `LoaderProps`.

Rendered as a pure-CSS bordered-ring spinner (`1.1s ease` rotation, ported
verbatim from the vendored animation timing) — **DEVIATION**: the official
Loader is an animated inline SVG glyph; this package never ships inline SVG.
`role="status"` + `aria-label="Loading"` are this package's own addition (the
vendored bundle carries no ARIA at all).
```tsx
<Loader size="medium" color="color-icon-primary" />
```

### Icon
`IconProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children">`
- `name: string` (required — Material Icons glyph name)
- `size?: number` (px, default `20`) — snapped to the nearest `--hx-size-icon-*` step (12/14/16/18/20/24/28/32/40/48/56/60/64); never sets an inline `fontSize`.
- `color?: "primary" | "secondary" | "tertiary" | "neutral" | "subtle" | "inverse" | "weak" | "accent" | "accent-hover" | "success" | "warning" | "error" | "rating"`
- `ariaLabel?: string` — when set, the icon is exposed to assistive tech (`aria-hidden` is otherwise always `"true"`).
```tsx
<Icon name="delete" size={16} color="error" />
```

### Avatar
`AvatarProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children">`
- `src?: string`
- `name: string` (required — used for alt text, the initials fallback, AND to pick a colour)
- `size?: "sm" | "md"` (default `"md"`)

There is no `color`/`variant` prop. `Avatar` deterministically auto-picks one
of the **9 real colour sets** (`--hx-color-avatar-{one..nine}-{background,border,text}`)
from `name`: sums `name`'s UTF-16 char codes, takes the result modulo 9.
```tsx
<Avatar name="Ada Lovelace" size="sm" />
```

### AvatarGroup
`AvatarGroupProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "children">`
- `avatars: AvatarGroupItem[]` (required) where `AvatarGroupItem extends Omit<AvatarProps, "size" | "id"> { id?: string | number }`
- `size?: "l" | "m" | "s"` (default `"l"`)
- `maxVisible?: number` (default `3`) — remaining avatars collapse into a `+N` overflow chip.
- `ariaLabel?: string`
```tsx
<AvatarGroup avatars={[{ name: "Eve" }, { name: "Kim" }, { name: "Xia" }, { name: "Dana" }]} maxVisible={3} />
```

### Badge
**BREAKING (0.3.x): the old single `<Badge variant="dot|count|tag" count={n}>`
component is gone**, split into the official DS's five-member Badge family:
`Badge` (this entry, the semantic pill), `BadgeDot`, `BadgeCount`, `BadgeTag`,
`BadgeShift` (below). Old call sites must migrate to the matching new component.

`BadgeProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children">`
- `children: React.ReactNode` (required)
- `variant?: "neutral" | "accent" | "success" | "warning" | "error"` (default `"neutral"`)
- `ariaLabel?: string`
```tsx
<Badge variant="success">Active</Badge>
```

### BadgeDot
`BadgeDotProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children">`
- `background?: BadgeBackgroundToken` (default `"error"`)
- `ariaLabel?: string`

`BadgeBackgroundToken = "primary" | "secondary" | "tertiary" | "strong" | "neutral" | "subtle" | "weak" | "page" | "hoverlist" | "inverse" | "accent" | "accent-hover" | "highlight" | "success" | "warning" | "error"` — maps 1:1 to `--hx-color-background-<suffix>`.
```tsx
<BadgeDot background="success" ariaLabel="Online" />
```

### BadgeCount
`BadgeCountProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children">`
- `value: string | number` (required)
- `background?: BadgeBackgroundToken` (default `"error"`)
- `ariaLabel?: string`

Single-digit values render as a fixed 16×16 circle; multi-digit values render
a growing pill.
```tsx
<BadgeCount value={5} />
```

### BadgeTag
`BadgeTagProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children">`
- `type?: "new" | "beta"` (default `"new"`)
- `tone?: "light" | "dark"` (default `"light"`)
- `children?: React.ReactNode` — omitted, falls back to `"Beta"`/`"New"` by `type`.
- `ariaLabel?: string`
```tsx
<BadgeTag type="beta" tone="dark" />
```

### BadgeShift
`BadgeShiftProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children">`
- `status?: "online" | "offline"` (default `"offline"`)
- `size?: "xl" | "l" | "m" | "s"` (default `"xl"` — 24×24/16×16/12×12/8×8 px)
- `tooltipContent?: string` — wraps the indicator in `Tooltip` only when set.
- `tooltipPlacement?: "top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right"` (default `"top"`)
- `ariaLabel?: string`
```tsx
<BadgeShift status="online" tooltipContent="Online now" />
```

## Extensions (not in @hubex/design-system)

The 13 components below are this package's **own additions** — they exist
because HubEx plugin UI needs them, but they have **no counterpart in
`@hubex/design-system@0.4.0`** (confirmed by their absence from
`tools/.official-ds-ref/package/dist/esm/src/components/`, the vendored
reference used to verify the official-vs-extension split). Their props are
this package's own design, still governed by "Anti-invention rules" above
(no hardcoded values — every class they render pulls from the same `--hx-*`
token set), but there is no upstream API to realign them to.

### Alert
`AlertProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "title">`
- `severity?: "info" | "success" | "warning" | "danger"` (default `"info"`)
- `title?: string`
```tsx
<Alert severity="danger" title="Error">Something went wrong.</Alert>
```

### Breadcrumbs
`BreadcrumbsProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>, "children">`
- `items: BreadcrumbItem[]` (required) where `BreadcrumbItem = { label: string; href?: string }`
```tsx
<Breadcrumbs items={[{ label: "Home", href: "/" }, { label: "Settings" }]} />
```

### Calendar
`CalendarProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "onChange">`
- `value?: Date`
- `onChange: (date: Date) => void` (required)
- `month?: Date` — controls the displayed month, independent of `value`.
```tsx
<Calendar value={date} onChange={setDate} />
```

### Chip
`ChipProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `onRemove?: () => void`
```tsx
<Chip onRemove={() => removeFilter("status")}>Status: Open</Chip>
```

### DatePicker
`DatePickerProps extends Omit<React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "value" | "onChange" | "type" | "readOnly">`
- `value?: Date`
- `onChange: (date: Date) => void` (required)
- `placeholder?: string`

Trigger is a read-only `Input`; the `Calendar` popover renders into a portal
positioned from the trigger's measured bounding box.
```tsx
<DatePicker value={date} onChange={setDate} placeholder="Pick a date" />
```

### Drawer
`DrawerProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `open: boolean` (required)
- `onClose: () => void` (required)
- `side?: "right" | "left"` (default `"right"`)
```tsx
<Drawer open={isOpen} onClose={() => setOpen(false)} side="right">
  Panel content
</Drawer>
```

### Field
`FieldProps`: `{ label?: string; htmlFor?: string; className?: string; children?: React.ReactNode }`
```tsx
<Field label="Email" htmlFor="email">
  <Input id="email" />
</Field>
```

### Label
`LabelProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>` (no extra props — thin `<span className="hx-label">` wrapper, same visual as `Field`'s form label, for standalone inline label text)
```tsx
<Label>Optional</Label>
```

### Menu
`MenuProps extends Omit<React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>, "children" | "type" | "aria-haspopup" | "aria-expanded">`
- `trigger: React.ReactNode` (required)
- `items: MenuItem[]` (required) where `MenuItem = { label: string; onSelect: () => void }`
- `className?: string` — applies to the portalled item list, not the trigger.
```tsx
<Menu trigger={<Button>Actions</Button>} items={[{ label: "Edit", onSelect: onEdit }]} />
```

### Modal
`ModalProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "title">`
- `open: boolean` (required)
- `onClose: () => void` (required)
- `title?: string`
```tsx
<Modal open={isOpen} onClose={() => setOpen(false)} title="Confirm">
  Are you sure?
</Modal>
```

### Tabs
`TabsProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "onChange" | "children">`
- `value: string` (required)
- `onChange: (value: string) => void` (required)
- `items: TabItem[]` (required) where `TabItem = { value: string; label: string }`
```tsx
<Tabs value={tab} onChange={setTab} items={[{ value: "a", label: "A" }, { value: "b", label: "B" }]} />
```

### Table (+ Table.Head / Table.Row / Table.Cell / Table.HeadCell)
`TableProps extends React.TableHTMLAttributes<HTMLTableElement>`
`TableHeadProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLTableSectionElement>`
`TableRowProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLTableRowElement>`
`TableCellProps extends React.TdHTMLAttributes<HTMLTableCellElement>`
`TableHeadCellProps extends React.ThHTMLAttributes<HTMLTableCellElement>`
(no component-specific props beyond native attributes)
```tsx
<Table>
  <Table.Head>
    <Table.Row><Table.HeadCell>Name</Table.HeadCell></Table.Row>
  </Table.Head>
  <tbody>
    <Table.Row><Table.Cell>Alice</Table.Cell></Table.Row>
  </tbody>
</Table>
```

### Tag
`TagProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `color?: "neutral" | "brand" | "danger"` (default `"neutral"`)
```tsx
<Tag color="brand">New</Tag>
```

## Rules

DO:
- Import components from `@cvetkov_es/react` before hand-rolling any UI element —
  check this file first.
- `import "@cvetkov_es/css"` exactly once, at your app's entry point.
- Use `--hx-*` CSS variables (or the existing `.hx-*` classes) for any custom
  element that isn't covered by a component.
- Use the Roboto font stack, 13px base body size, and accent color `#32B4EB`
  — i.e. `var(--hx-font-family)`, `var(--hx-font-body-regular-size)` (or the
  legacy `var(--hx-font-size-base)` alias), `var(--hx-color-text-accent)`
  (or `-background-accent` / `-border-accent` / `-icon-accent`, depending on
  role) — rather than restating these literals.
- Load the Material Icons font yourself (aliased to family `"material"`) if
  you use `Icon` or any icon-accepting prop (`Button.icon`, `InputBase.leftIcon`, etc.).
- Check `packages/react/src/<Name>/<Name>.tsx` directly if you need to verify
  a prop this file might have gotten stale on — it is the single source of
  truth, this file is a snapshot of it.

DON'T:
- Don't hardcode colors, fonts, radii, or spacing — reference the tokens
  above instead.
- Don't import CSS from inside library/component code — `@cvetkov_es/react`
  intentionally ships no CSS of its own so consumers can tree-shake unused
  components; only the consumer app imports `@cvetkov_es/css`, once.
- Don't `require("@cvetkov_es/react")` or `require("@cvetkov_es/css")` — both packages
  are ESM-only (`"type": "module"`, no CJS build). Use `import`.
- Don't pass an SVG render-prop to an `icon`/`leftIcon`/`icon`-shaped prop —
  every icon slot in this package is a Material Icons glyph *name* string,
  never a component/render-function, even where the official
  `@hubex/design-system` API takes one.
- Don't read `docs/design-source/figma-variables.{json,txt}` for current
  values or props — it's a historical record of the pre-rebase Figma source,
  superseded by `@hubex/design-system@0.4.0` everywhere in this file.

### Good vs bad

Good — destructive actions use a normal `primary` button, no variant needed:
```tsx
<Button onClick={onDelete}>Delete</Button>
```
Bad — hand-rolled red button with inline hex: there is no `"danger"` variant
and no red button anywhere in HubEx, so this both ignores the design system
AND invents a control that doesn't exist in the real design:
```tsx
<button style={{ background: "#ED1940", color: "#fff", borderRadius: 4 }} onClick={onDelete}>
  Delete
</button>
```

Good — a one-off custom element still styled from tokens:
```tsx
<div style={{
  padding: "var(--hx-spacing-x4)",
  borderRadius: "var(--hx-border-radius-medium)",
  background: "var(--hx-color-background-subtle)",
  color: "var(--hx-color-text-secondary)",
  fontFamily: "var(--hx-font-family)",
  fontSize: "var(--hx-font-body-regular-size)"
}}>
  Empty state
</div>
```
Bad — same element with hardcoded values that will drift from the design system:
```tsx
<div style={{ padding: 16, borderRadius: 5, background: "#EEEEEE", color: "#777777", fontFamily: "Arial", fontSize: 13 }}>
  Empty state
</div>
```

Good — the new `Select` combobox API (`options` + `value`/`onChange`):
```tsx
<Select options={[{ value: "us", label: "US" }]} value={country} onChange={(v) => setCountry(v)} />
```
Bad — the old 0.2.x children-based API no longer compiles (`options` is
required, `<option>` children are not read):
```tsx
<Select value={country} onChange={(e) => setCountry(e.target.value)}>
  <option value="us">US</option>
</Select>
```
